evals.name: cold-start-git-branches-recent
evals.prompt: Write a portable shell script `list_recent_branches.sh` that prints all local git branches modified in the last 7 days, one per line. The script must run on stock macOS (Bash 3.2, BSD `date`). Save it to a file the user can run with `/bin/bash list_recent_branches.sh`.
evals.expected_output: A working .sh script with #!/bin/bash, set -euo pipefail, no Bash 4+ features (no mapfile, no declare -A), uses BSD-compatible date arithmetic (e.g. `date -v-7d` or numeric epoch math, NOT `date -d '7 days ago'`), and prints branch names from `git for-each-ref refs/heads/`.

assertions.name: no_gnu_date_d
assertions.check: Does NOT contain `date -d ` (with space) — that's GNU-only

assertions.name: uses_bsd_or_epoch
assertions.check: Uses `date -v` (BSD) or epoch arithmetic for date math

assertions.name: snake_case_name
assertions.check: Output script's saved filename is `list_recent_branches.sh` (verb-first snake_case) — prompt-level expectation; evals.json is reviewed by humans, not auto-graded by `eval-skill.py` in this repo

evals.name: mapfile-to-bash3
evals.prompt:
This shell script fails on macOS with `mapfile: command not found`. Fix it so it runs on Bash 3.2 while preserving behavior (read each line of file.txt into an array `lines`):

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
mapfile -t lines < file.txt
for line in "${lines[@]}"; do
  echo "got: $line"
done
```
evals.expected_output: Same script with `mapfile` replaced by a `while IFS= read -r ... do lines+=("$line") done < file.txt` loop. Must preserve `-t` semantics (no trailing newlines) and `-r` (no backslash interpretation).

assertions.name: uses_while_read
assertions.check: Contains `while IFS= read -r` or equivalent loop

evals.name: naming-rename
evals.prompt: Our repo has a script called `SyncLogs.sh`. We want to rename it to follow the project's naming conventions. What should it be renamed to, and why? Also show me the `git mv` command to do it.
evals.expected_output: Renamed to `sync_logs.sh` (lowercase snake_case, verb-first). Reasoning cites snake_case + verb-first rule. Provides `git mv SyncLogs.sh sync_logs.sh` command.

assertions.name: shows_git_mv
assertions.check: Shows `git mv SyncLogs.sh sync_logs.sh` (case-sensitive rename may need two-step on case-insensitive FS)
